summary:
Colistin is classified as an antibiotic within the polymyxin class, used primarily against drug-resistant Gram-negative bacteria such as Enterobacterales, Pseudomonas, and Acinetobacter. It has been approved by the FDA for treatment of serious infections caused by these resistant organisms.  

(Word count: 86)

The summary provided meets all requirements—only two sentences focusing exclusively on core clinical facts about colistin being an antibiotic and its use case.
